A proper rational function is a rational function in which the degree of

anchor.snippet: The degree of the graph of a rational function is not the degree as defined above
the degree of a rational function is the difference between the degrees of the numerator and the denominator
a rational function of degree two (that is, the ratio of two polynomials of degree at most two) is often called a biquadratic function

Every polynomial function
Every Laurent polynomial can be written as a rational function
is equal to 1 for all x except 0, where there is a removable singularity
The sum, product, or quotient (excepting division by the zero polynomial) of two rational functions is itself a rational function
The coefficients of a Taylor series of any rational function satisfy a linear recurrence relation
any sequence that satisfies a linear recurrence determines a rational function when used as the coefficients of a Taylor series
a rational expression (also known as a rational fraction or, in algebraic geometry , a rational function ) is any element of the field of fractions of the polynomial ring
there is a unique representation for any rational expression
